title: basej-wgs pipeline parameters
description: Single-cell WGS/WES QC pipeline with alignment and metrics collection.

input_output_options.title: Input/output options
input_output_options.description: Define where the pipeline should find input data and save output data.

input_csv.description: Path to comma-separated file containing information about the samples in the experiment.
input_csv.help_text: You will need to create a design file with information about the samples in your experiment before running the pipeline. Use this parameter to specify its location. It must be a comma-separated file with at least 3 columns: biosampleName, read1, read2 (or biosampleName, cram for Ultima), and a header row. Optional column `custom_mean_coverage` (float) is required when `coverage_target` is set; it is used to compute the per-row subsample proportion.

workspace.description: Workspace identifier for S3 output layout and tagging.
workspace.help_text: Used in S3 output prefixes and applied as an S3 object tag. Required for standardized output organization.

dataset_id.description: Dataset identifier for S3 tagging and organization.
dataset_id.help_text: Applied as an S3 object tag to associate outputs with a specific dataset.

resume_workflow_id.description: Original workflow_id to reuse when resuming a run, keeping output paths stable and idempotent.
resume_workflow_id.help_text: Set this to the workflow_id of a previous run when resuming so outputs are written to the same S3 location and duplication is avoided. When unset, workflow_id is derived from TOWER_WORKFLOW_ID.

workflow_id.description: Workflow run identifier. Auto-populated from TOWER_WORKFLOW_ID or Nextflow session ID.
workflow_id.help_text: Unique identifier for this workflow run. Used in S3 output paths and tags. Defaults to TOWER_WORKFLOW_ID environment variable if running on Seqera Platform, otherwise uses the Nextflow session ID.

outputDir.description: Output directory for pipeline results. Use an s3:// URI for production runs; relative or absolute local paths are accepted for local/test runs.
outputDir.help_text: Production launches should target an s3:// path (e.g., 's3://your-output-bucket/analysis/project_name/pipeline-basej-wgs'). Local nf-test runs may use a relative path like 'results'.

timestamp.help_text: Optional timestamp for naming execution reports. Format: 'yyMMdd_HHmmss'.

reference_genome_options.title: Reference genome options
reference_genome_options.description: Define the reference genome and associated files for alignment.

genome.description: Name of the reference genome to use for alignment.
genome.help_text: Name of the reference genome. This will automatically configure the reference FASTA and associated files. Supported genomes: GRCh38, GRCh37, GRCm39, ARSUCD2.

reference.description: Path to reference genome FASTA directory. Auto-populated from genomes.config; override only if you know what you are doing.

base_metrics_intervals.description: Path to BED file with intervals for base metrics collection (WGS mode). Auto-populated from genomes.config.

pipeline_mode_options.title: Pipeline mode options
pipeline_mode_options.description: Options to control WGS vs WES mode.

mode.description: Pipeline mode: 'wgs' for whole genome sequencing or 'exome' for whole exome sequencing.
mode.help_text: Controls which metrics are collected and which intervals are used. WGS mode runs WgsMetricsAlgo on genome-wide intervals. Exome mode runs CoverageMetrics on target intervals and runs Picard CollectHsMetrics. When mode='exome', exome_panel is required.

exome_panel.description: Exome panel identifier. Required when mode=exome; ignored when mode=wgs.
exome_panel.help_text: The exome panel key from genomes.config. Must match a panel defined under the chosen genome. For GRCh38: 'xGen Exome Hyb Panel v2', 'TruSight One', 'TWIST', 'Agilent Clinical Exome', 'Twist Exome 2.0', 'xGen Pan-Cancer Hybridization Panel', 'Twist Alliance CNTG Hereditary Oncology Panel'.
enum: Twist Alliance CNTG Hereditary Oncology Panel

skip_subsampling.description: Skip read subsampling and use all reads for alignment.
skip_subsampling.help_text: When true, all FASTQ reads are aligned without subsampling. When false (default), reads are counted (zcat + wc); if total reads across mates exceed max_total_reads, seqkit sample caps at that total; otherwise inputs pass through unchanged. Has no effect on the CRAM branch — use coverage_target for CRAM subsampling.

max_total_reads.description: Maximum total reads (sum of mates for paired-end) before subsampling applies.
max_total_reads.help_text: When skip_subsampling=false, if total reads exceed this value, seqkit sample reduces to this many reads (same proportion per mate). No subsampling if total is at or below this value.

seqkit_sample_seed.help_text: Seed passed to seqkit sample -s. Use the same seed to reproduce identical subsamples.

samtools_seed.description: Seed for reproducible samtools CRAM subsampling.
samtools_seed.help_text: Seed passed to samtools view -s for CRAM subsampling. Use the same seed to reproduce identical subsamples.

coverage_target.description: Target coverage (x) for Ultima/CRAM subsampling. One target per pipeline run.
coverage_target.help_text: When set, every CRAM biosample is subsampled to this coverage and emitted as `{biosampleName}_cov{NN}x`. Requires a `custom_mean_coverage` column in input_csv per CRAM row. If a biosample's `custom_mean_coverage` is at or below `coverage_target` (cannot upsample), it is passed through at its native coverage and labeled with its actual floored coverage (e.g. a 9.6x sample becomes `{biosampleName}_cov09x`) so it is not pooled with true `coverage_target`-x samples downstream. Has no effect on the FASTQ branch — only effective when platform=Ultima with CRAM input. Run multiple coverage tiers as separate pipeline launches (e.g. 5x, 10x, 15x = 3 launches). Default: null (no subsampling).
coverage_target.x-agent-hint: The parameter is named `coverage_target` (not `target_coverage`). Only effective for CRAM input on platform=Ultima.

platform.description: Sequencing platform used for the data.
platform.help_text: The sequencing platform identifier. Used in the read group header of the output BAM file. Use 'Ultima' for CRAM input.

architecture.help_text: The CPU architecture for AWS Batch compute. Use 'arm' for ARM-based instances (Graviton, recommended) or 'x86' for Intel/AMD.

pipeline_tool.help_text: Select the alignment and metrics tool stack. 'sentieon' uses the proprietary Sentieon tools (requires a valid Sentieon license). 'opensource' uses BWA-MEM2 + samtools markdup + Picard (no license required, x86-only).

resource_label_options.title: Resource labelling options
resource_label_options.description: Tags applied to AWS Batch jobs for cost tracking. Auto-populated by the launching platform; rarely set by users.

pipeline_user.description: Human-readable user identifier applied as an AWS Batch resource label.

biosample_count.description: Number of biosamples in the run; used as an AWS Batch resource label for cost tracking.

basejumper_organization_uuid.description: BaseJumper organization UUID applied as an AWS Batch resource label.

basejumper_organization_name.description: BaseJumper organization name applied as an AWS Batch resource label.
